pain: Windows environment requires an additional connector/layer to run the agentic stack inside Warp (Linux environment). Setup guidance currently fragmented across multiple Circle posts.
Did not finish the recommended file structure + MCP setup checklist in the first week. Downstream effect: slower onboarding, increased reliance on synchronous 1-on-1 sessions.

Setup script assumed Unix-style sudo; Windows + Warp required bash.exe, run-as-admin, and Perl+curl workarounds. Rebuilt setup script promised. Also navigated spaces-in-folder-path issues in the terminal.

Windows users hit multi-hour setup friction installing Claude Code via WSL: Linux sub-environment install, Ubuntu default shell change, Node/NVM install, reboot required, firewall/network blocks causing SSL handshake failures, and network permissions needing manual mirror from Windows to WSL. Two separate members required live SA-support sessions (60+ minutes each) to complete setup.

New users struggle to remember the "claude --dangerously-skip-permissions" command that must be run on each fresh session. Users also struggle to set persistent working directory in Warp, requiring multiple settings navigations. High friction for non-technical members.

Once setup is complete, members hit an orientation gap — they have a working Claude Code but do not know what to do first. Clayton (agency owner) explicitly asked: "I don't even know where to begin." Members have pre-existing Claude projects and skills they cannot easily migrate into the agentic workflow. Need a "first session" guide with 3-5 concrete starter commands.

Multiple members require comprehensive guides and checklists for navigating command line basics and file structure concepts. This is distinct from the Windows/WSL installation issue (stack-004) — members who have successfully installed the stack still struggle with core terminal navigation and understanding how directories and file paths work in an agentic workflow. Team confirmed scheduling individual setup sessions to compensate for missing documentation.

Member was running Claude Code from the computer root directory (/Users/Jonathan/) instead of the canonical Mastermind SA project folder. Result: client files scattered across disk in multiple redundant folders, memory fragments stored inconsistently. Required a full session to reorganize. Pattern corroborates stack-007 (CLI/file structure gaps) and stack-006 (post-setup orientation gap) — working directory misconfiguration is a recurring onboarding failure mode.

MCPs are installed per-machine in ~/.claude/ (local config directory). File-sync tools like SyncThing only sync the project folder, not the hidden ~/.claude/ directory. Multi-machine agencies must manually reinstall MCPs on each device after initial setup.
